description: next/ v2.0 — injection molding machine (OT asset). Declares the full flat sim-v5 tag set delivered by the OPC source (sources/opcua/opcua-sgm-004-processdata.json and the aligned sgm event/telemetry/BDE sources): machine state + BDE counters, the injection process telemetry (hot-runner/nozzle/cavity/mould temps & pressures, injection speed, shot volume, cushion, cycle), mould/tooling, energy, and material/dryer/hopper. Source tag == profile attribute (1:1, end-to-end consistent), mirroring the CNC profile. The recipe_* constraints monitor the live process values (IST) against the active recipe SOLL band via valueFrom; equipment_* envelopes + the two-tier hot-runner control_limit are the machine-spec SSOT.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Actual machine status (RUNNING / IDLE / STOPPED / FAULT / MAINTENANCE / OFFLINE — sim-v5 vocabulary, identical to the CNC profile).

attributes.name: inCycle
attributes.description: Machine is in the injection cycle (cycle gate for pressure rules).

attributes.name: currentProgram
attributes.description: Active program / job on the machine.

attributes.name: plastProgress
attributes.description: Plasticising progress of the current cycle.

attributes.name: elapsedSec
attributes.description: Elapsed time in the current phase.

attributes.name: phaseName
attributes.description: Current Euromap-77 cycle phase of the injection cycle. THE ENUM IS THE POINT: a phase gate is only worth anything if the literal it compares against actually exists in the data. The previous description of this attribute ('inject / hold / plast / cool / open') was itself WRONG — lower-case, five phases, CLOSING and EJECT missing, COOL_PLAST mis-spelled — i.e. every literal it suggested would have silently matched NOTHING. The enum below is MEASURED against the running sim (2026-07-12): read live from opc.tcp://192.168.178.154:36063 node ns=1;s=Machine/typeSpecific/phase/name (StatusCode Good, value "CLOSING"), and cross-checked against the DEPLOYED artifact (image machines-sgm:2b21c128, /app/src/config.ts:106 `export type SgmCyclePhase = 'CLOSING' | 'INJECT' | 'HOLD' | 'COOL_PLAST' | 'OPENING' | 'EJECT'` and the FSM order array in /app/src/machine.ts:665). With this enum, ci/lint-constraints.mjs rejects any when/require predicate (op eq/ne/in) whose value is not one of these six — so the 'guard against a vocabulary that does not exist' bug is now caught by CI instead of by a customer.

attributes.name: mouldId
attributes.description: Mould / tool currently set up (= USES_TOOL tool_id).

constraints.name: recipe_hotrunner_temp_band
constraints.description: Recipe / setting parameter: hot-runner temperature must lie within the target band (resolved at runtime via valueFrom recipe:hotrunner_temp_band). Outside -> material degradation / dimensional deviation on the part.

constraints.name: recipe_part_mass_band
constraints.description: Recipe / setting parameter: part mass must lie within the target band (resolved at runtime via valueFrom recipe:part_mass_band; the recipe target is deliberately chosen narrower than the current actual to detect overfilling/drift early). Outside -> over-/under-injection, scrap. Cycle-gated (inCycle==true), since OUTSIDE the cycle no part is made: a partMass_g sample taken between shots belongs to no shot at all. Measured on sgm-004 (2026-07-12): of 901,142 partMass_g points, 581,521 (64 %) carry inCycle=false — idle-time values that, ungated, enter every population drawn from this rule (capability, control charts, deviation counts). BE HONEST ABOUT WHAT THIS FIXES: those idle values happen to carry the same distribution as the real shots (in-cycle n=319,621, mean 10.4001, sigma 0.0840; idle n=581,521, mean 10.3999, sigma 0.0839), so Cp/Ca do NOT move because of this gate. It is not a numbers fix — it makes the POPULATION honest, and every future sample drawn from it defensible.

constraints.name: recipe_cushion_band
constraints.description: Recipe / setting parameter: residual material cushion must lie within the target band (resolved at runtime via valueFrom recipe:cushion_band). Outside -> fluctuation in the hold-pressure profile, dimensional deviation.

constraints.name: recipe_peak_pressure_band
constraints.description: Recipe / setting parameter: peak injection pressure must lie within the target band (resolved at runtime via valueFrom recipe:peak_pressure_band). PHASE-gated (phaseName==HOLD) — NOT cycle-gated. THIS GATE IS A CORRECTION OF A FALSE ACCUSATION AGAINST THE MACHINE. The previous gate (inCycle==true) was ours and it was wrong: inCycle spans the WHOLE cycle including COOL_PLAST/OPENING/EJECT, where the injection pressure is physically 0 bar. The rule therefore drew its population from a set that is ~half zeros and reported the machine as massively incapable. Measured on sgm-004 (edge TS, 2026-07-12): gate inCycle -> mean 208.8 bar, sigma 259.2, Ca 5.385, only 17.6 % in band [450,560]; 24,804 of 50,616 gated samples (49.00 %) are EXACTLY 0 bar. Gate HOLD -> mean 557.6 bar, sigma 114.6, Ca 0.956, 86.5 % in band. The machine was injecting at ~558 bar against a band that ends at 560 — the deviation was an artefact of OUR population, not a fault of the customer's machine. Independently corroborated against the DEPLOYED sim FSM (machines-sgm:2b21c128, /app/src/machine.ts:651-700 + config.ts:147): the sim holds ONE currentPressureBar and publishes peakPressureBar = p*1.05, holdPressureBar = p*0.7. Per phase p approaches: CLOSING->0, INJECT->peakBar (ramp, 200 bar/s, dwell only 6 % of cycle = 1.4 s, so it never plateaus), HOLD->holdBar (=peakBar*0.5, dwell 18 % of cycle), COOL_PLAST/OPENING/EJECT->0. Replaying that FSM with sgm-004's real config (cycleTimeSec 24, injectionPressureBar 1000) reproduces the measurement: gate inCycle -> mean 170.5, 51.8 % zeros, 18.5 % in band; gate HOLD -> mean 516.6, sigma 16.8, 0 % zeros, 100 % in band. HOLD is the ONLY phase in which this signal has a plateau, hence the only phase in which a BAND on it is a meaningful statement; INJECT (mean 315, 0 % in band) is a transient ramp and would be an equally wrong gate.

constraints.name: recipe_hold_pressure_band
constraints.description: Recipe / setting parameter: hold pressure must lie within the target band (resolved at runtime via valueFrom recipe:hold_pressure_band). PHASE-gated (phaseName==HOLD) — NOT cycle-gated, for exactly the reason given on recipe_peak_pressure_band: inCycle includes ejection and opening, where the pressure is physically 0 bar, so the rule was accusing the machine of a deviation that OUR population had manufactured. Measured on sgm-004 (edge TS, 2026-07-12): gate inCycle -> mean 139.2 bar; gate HOLD -> mean 371.7 bar against band [300,380]. Corroborated on the deployed FSM replay (see recipe_peak_pressure_band): gate inCycle -> mean 113.7, 51.8 % zeros, 18.5 % in band [300,380]; gate HOLD -> mean 344.4, sigma 11.2, 0 % zeros, 100 % in band. HOLD is the phase in which the hold pressure is by definition being held — it is the only population about which this band makes a claim.

constraints.name: equipment_hotrunner_temp_envelope
constraints.description: Equipment / asset-per-se: hot-runner temperature must stay within the machine's safe operating envelope, independent of the running recipe. Outside [180,270] degC -> heater / thermocouple / controller fault (a machine problem regardless of the article). Deliberately wider than the recipe band on the same attribute. LITERAL value = machine-spec SSOT — adjust to the machine spec.

constraints.name: equipment_peak_pressure_limit
constraints.description: Equipment / asset-per-se: peak injection pressure must stay below the machine's safe mechanical/hydraulic limit during the active cycle, independent of the running recipe. Above [0,1000] bar -> overpressure / machine fault (not a recipe deviation). Cycle-gated (inCycle==true). LITERAL value = machine-spec SSOT — adjust to the machine spec.

constraints.name: hotrunner_control_limits
constraints.description: Control limits (Warngrenze + Eingriffsgrenze) for the hot-runner temperature — a two-tier escalation band on top of the recipe SOLL. Inside the warn band [225,234] degC = ok; outside warn -> 'warning'-tier alarm (watch); outside the action band [218,241] -> 'error'-tier alarm (intervene). Both tiers stay inside the equipment envelope [180,270] (equipment_hotrunner_temp_envelope). LITERAL bands = static SSOT for the pilot; switch to valueFrom 'recipe:hotrunner_limits' to source them per active recipe. Drives the OT alarm panel with a raise/clear lifecycle (auto-clears when the value returns inside the warn band).

constraints.name: recipe_mould_temp_band
constraints.description: Recipe / setting parameter: mouldTempC must lie within the active recipe band (valueFrom recipe:mould_temp_band — since 2026-07-12 the PA66 band [80,90] degC around SOLL 85, see the recipe's toleranceSource=norm). Cycle-gated (inCycle==true) and DELIBERATELY NOT phase-gated, unlike the two pressure bands: the mould temperature is a slow THERMAL STATE of the tool, not a per-phase event. It is a meaningful measurement in every phase of a running cycle (the tool does not cool down between INJECT and EJECT), so inCycle is the honest population here — narrowing it to HOLD would throw away ~82 % of the valid samples for no gain. What inCycle DOES correctly exclude is the time when no recipe is in force: between shots and after shift end (measured 2026-07-12: with all SGMs idle, mouldTempC reads exactly 25.00 degC ambient, sigma 0.00 — the tool is simply cold), where a band violation would be a deviation against a recipe that is not running. NOTE ON CAPABILITY: measured on sgm-004 (2026-07-12, over the FULL ungated series) mouldTempC is by far the least stationary monitored signal, sigma_total/sigma_short = 156.4. The deployed sim warms the tool with approach(mouldTempC, target, 0.1 degC/s), i.e. a ~10-minute warm-up ramp from ambient; the inCycle gate does NOT remove that ramp. A Cp over a warming tool is meaningless, and capability.max_stationarity_ratio in the recipe — not this gate — stays the authority on whether a Cp may be reported at all.

constraints.name: equipment_mould_temp_envelope
constraints.description: Equipment / asset-per-se (ASSUMED machine-spec default): mould temperature must stay within the machine cooling/heating envelope [15,120] degC. CHECKED (not assumed) against the PA66 re-calibration of 2026-07-12 and DELIBERATELY LEFT UNCHANGED: the envelope is a property of the machine (what the tempering unit can physically do), not of the recipe running on it. The new recipe band [80,90] and the machine's actual 87.5 degC both sit comfortably inside [15,120], so this constraint stays correct and keeps doing its job — it remains the outer, recipe-independent bound that the recipe band and the control limits [78,92] must both nest inside, which they do.

constraints.name: equipment_dryer_temp_envelope
constraints.description: Equipment / asset-per-se (ASSUMED machine-spec default): resin dryer temperature must stay within [60,130] degC.

constraints.name: equipment_cooling_flow_min
constraints.description: Equipment / asset-per-se (ASSUMED): mould cooling-water flow must be >= 5 L/min during the active cycle, else overheating risk.

constraints.name: mould_temp_control_limits
constraints.description: Control limits (Warngrenze + Eingriffsgrenze) for mould temperature: warn band [82,88], action band [78,92] degC; both inside the equipment envelope [15,120] (equipment_mould_temp_envelope, unchanged). PULLED ALONG with the PA66 recipe band: these limits were [42,58]/[38,62], i.e. centred on the old — and materially wrong — 50 degC SOLL. A PA66 tool runs at 80-90 degC. Had the recipe band moved to [80,90] and these limits stayed put, the OT alarm panel would have raised a permanent, un-clearable action alarm at every single sample (a real mould at 87.5 degC sits 25 degC outside the old action band) — a rule contradicting its own recipe. The offsets from the recipe band are preserved exactly as they were designed: warn = band tightened by 2 degC on each side, action = band widened by 2 degC on each side (old: band [40,60] -> warn [42,58] / action [38,62]; new: band [80,90] -> warn [82,88] / action [78,92]).

constraints.name: power_positive_when_running
constraints.description: Machine is in the injection cycle but draws 0 kW — sensor / edge-communication fault (equipment).
